name: MDC (anoxic M9-derived coculture minimal medium) (PMC12997748)
description: MDC (anoxic M9-derived coculture minimal medium) — medium reported in PMC12997748, extracted for GrowthDB and mapped to BiGG.

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC12997748 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Composition mapped to BiGG (mapper + salt dissociation); presence-based bounds; paper_amount retained. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.base_expansion: Base 'M9' expanded to standard composition: M9 minimal medium (Sambrook & Russell 2001): Na2HPO4, KH2PO4, NaCl, NH4Cl, MgSO4, CaCl2 + carbon source.
provenance.verification_evidence: Media supplements were then added by syringe to the following final concentrations from anoxic 100x stock solutions: glucose, 25 mM; NH4Cl, 10 mM; MgSO4, 1 mM; CaCl2, 0.1 mM.
provenance.base_medium: MDC = anoxic minimal M9-derived coculture medium of LaSarre et al. 2017 (ISME J 11:337-348), re-used via Sangani et al. 2019 (FEMS Microbiol Lett 366:fnz162; ref 63 of this paper). This paper adds a MOPS buffer (100 or 200 mM, pH 7.0) and prepares it anoxically under N2. Trace-element solution is that of Kremer et al. 2015 (PNAS 112:2222-2226).

sources_chased.what: target paper methods (PMC12997748 / bioRxiv 2025.08.29.673078 / AEM aem.01745-25): MDC supplements, MOPS buffer, N2 anoxic prep
ref 63 = Sangani, McCully, LaSarre, McKinlay 2019, FEMS Microbiol Lett 366:fnz162, doi:10.1093/femsle/fnz162 (source cited for MDC)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch doi.org -> academic.oup.com; confirms MDC and points to LaSarre 2017 as the full recipe source

sources_chased.what: MDC full base recipe = LaSarre, Deutschbauer, Love, McKinlay 2017, ISME J 11:337-348, doi:10.1038/ismej.2016.141 (Nature paywalled; OUP mirror open)
trace-element solution = Kremer, LaSarre, Posto, McKinlay 2015, PNAS 112:2222-2226, doi:10.1073/pnas.1420663112; full composition (incl. 'Metals 44') reproduced in same-group patent WO2016109286A1
sources_chased.how: PNAS 403; recipe recovered verbatim from Google Patents WO2016109286A1 (same authors, identical defined medium and trace solution)
added: Metals 44: ZnSO4, MnSO4, CuSO4, Co(NO3)2, Na2B4O7, EDTA, FeSO4

recuration.notes: Anoxic defined minimal medium (N2 headspace, sealed serum vials/tubes). The current model list captured Ca, Cl, Co, Cu, Fe, K, Mg, Mn, Na, NH4, Pi, SO4, Zn and MOPS but was missing molybdate (Mo) and borate (B) - both present in the Kremer 2015 trace solution - plus thiosulfate, the PABA vitamin, and the N2 gas phase. Bulk phosphate is Na2HPO4 42.5 mM + KH2PO4 22 mM (M9-derived, from LaSarre 2017); sulfate comes chiefly from the 1 mM MgSO4 supplement. Trace-metal salts sit in a 0.1% v/v solution (and 'Metals 44' a further 0.1% v/v within it), so final metal concentrations are extremely low; st
